name: Kurtokha

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Kurtokha (kurt1248).

notes: Skeleton. Kurtokha (Kurtop) is an East Bodish (Sino-Tibetan) language of Lhuntse District in Bhutan, written in the Tibetan (Uchen) script. Tibetan orthography is a historical abugida whose spelling reflects Classical Tibetan rather than the modern spoken form: silent prefix/superscript/subscript letters, unwritten tone, and large sound changes between spelling and pronunciation mean a letter-by-letter reading does not yield the spoken IPA. Correct grapheme-to-IPA needs a per-word pronunciation stratum (a spelling-to-reading lexicon for this variety) that the engine does not bundle, so graphemes are left empty and coverage is effectively lexicon-dependent. Tone and the silent-letter/stack structure are not recoverable from the script alone.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Kurtokha (kurt1248)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
